title: Mere Christianity
author: C.S. Lewis
description: A reasoned defence of the Christian faith — belief, morality, and the nature of Christ explained with brilliant clarity.

passages.text: A man who was merely a man and said the sort of things Jesus said would not be a great moral teacher. He would either be a lunatic or else he would be the Devil of Hell. You must make your choice.
Pride is the great sin — the sin that was the beginning of evil. Pride is essentially competitive — it is not having things that pleases the proud, but having more than the next person.

title: Halley's Bible Handbook
author: Henry H. Halley
description: The authoritative guide to every book of the Bible — history, archaeology, maps and detailed notes on every passage.

passages.text: The Bible is the most wonderful book in the world. It is God's Word — His revealed will for mankind. To understand it is to know God Himself.

title: Spiritual Leadership
author: J. Oswald Sanders
description: The classic text on leading God's way — humility, prayer, discipline and sacrifice as the hallmarks of the servant leader.

passages.text: The spiritual leader of today is not one who simply manages people — he is one who has heard from God and leads others into God's purposes. Prayer is not preparation for the work; prayer is the work.

title: The Purpose Driven Church
author: Rick Warren
description: How to grow a healthy church built on five New Testament purposes that every congregation must fulfil.

passages.text: The issue is church health, not church growth. If your church is healthy, growth will occur naturally.

title: The Pursuit of God
author: A.W. Tozer
description: A burning call to deeper intimacy with God — perhaps the most beloved devotional classic of the twentieth century.

passages.text: We pursue God because and only because He has first put an urge within us that spurs us to the pursuit.

title: Knowing God
author: J.I. Packer
description: One of the greatest books of modern Christianity — a transforming exploration of the character and ways of God.
toc: The People Who Know Their God

passages.text: We are cruel to ourselves if we try to live in this world without knowing about the God whose world it is.

title: Prayer: Awe and Intimacy
author: Timothy Keller
description: A deeply practical and theological guide to prayer, weaving together Augustine, Luther, Calvin and the Psalms.

passages.text: Prayer is the only entryway into genuine self-knowledge. It is also the main way we experience deep change — the reordering of our loves.

title: Evidence That Demands a Verdict
author: Josh McDowell
description: The definitive apologetics handbook — historical, legal and scientific evidence for Scripture and the resurrection.

passages.text: The resurrection is either the greatest miracle or the greatest delusion history records. The evidence demands a verdict.

title: Experiencing God
author: Henry Blackaby
description: Knowing and doing the will of God — a transformative study of how to recognise God's activity and join Him in it.

passages.text: Watch to see where God is working and join Him there. When you see the Father at work — that is your invitation.

title: The Christian Family
author: Larry Christenson
description: A practical, scripture-grounded guide to building a God-centred home — marriage, parenting, authority and love.

passages.text: A Christian home is not an accident — it is a work of deliberate construction.

title: End Times Made Simple
author: Samuel Waldron
description: A clear, balanced introduction to biblical eschatology — the second coming, the millennium, judgment and the new creation.

passages.text: The purpose of prophecy is not to satisfy our curiosity about dates and events, but to purify our lives in the light of eternity.

title: The Screwtape Letters
author: C.S. Lewis
description: A brilliant satirical masterpiece — advice from a senior demon to a junior one on how to tempt a human soul away from God.

passages.text: Humans live in time but our Enemy destines them to eternity. Our business is to distract them from the present, either into the past or future.
